mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding its 12-quarter moving average signals underlying economic expansion, which improves corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in above-average GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.5%, it indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and reduces recession fears, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls reflect synchronized growth in the construction sector and broader employment, amplifying aggregate demand expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 40% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, further increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate and signaling restrictive policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL is above 3.0%, or the negative correlation breaks down over two consecutive tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 1.0% signals robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts industrial sector earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered months, or the signal predictive power vanishes when adjusted for seasonal factors over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales revenue combined with accelerating consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull inflation that erodes real purchasing power and prompts fears of aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day window.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce negative forward returns in more than 45% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects continuous labor market expansion, which supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in months with positive payroll growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate is actively rising above 2.0%, the cost of capital increases for leveraged firms and growth stocks, compressing P/E multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS increases while above 2.0%, or the negative predictive relationship reverses during two consecutive rate-hiking cycles.
